behaviors.description: Conversation history sent to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is at module handoff (history builder -> LLM request payload), not pure algorithm alone. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; a fake LLM client that captures payload is enough. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on what is sent. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text focusing on trimming before call, not on real infra or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the chat orchestrator with a fake LLM transport that records request context. Feed >10 prior messages and assert only the latest 10 are passed, in order, while under-limit histories are unchanged.

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer question instead of generic/off-topic output.
behaviors.justification: CONTENT-QUALITY PRIORITY + LLM-DEP: this directly evaluates response relevance/quality, which depends on real model output; deterministic stubs would hide the failure. MULTI-STEP: single generation quality check, so Agentic not Workflow. Rejection gate: Integration is disqualified because behavior text is about semantic correctness of generated content, not just routing/retries.
behaviors.test_description: Run real model calls on a fixed prompt set of customer questions and score whether answers are on-topic and question-addressing via rubric/judge criteria; fail when relevance drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects overly formal tone for casual-tier users and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: 'correctly identifies too formal' depends on model judgment (or model-based checker quality). MULTI-STEP: requires sequential agentic steps (initial generation -> tone assessment -> regeneration) and validating conditional handoff. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing tone decisions can miss real misclassification/regeneration behavior. Rejection gate: Agentic alone is insufficient because behavior explicitly includes downstream trigger/regeneration sequence, not only one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real LLM/tone-check path on casual-tier scenarios likely to produce formal drafts; assert formal first draft is flagged and a second generation is produced, with trace proving both steps occurred in sequence.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, initial response, regenerations) with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in module interaction (chat flow -> persistence layer schema/transaction). REAL-INFRA: local DB integration is sufficient; no requirement for real external network/OS behavior. LLM-DEP: content quality is irrelevant; deterministic generated text can be injected. MULTI-STEP: includes multiple records but not multiple agentic decisions for correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow against a real local test DB (transactional/ephemeral). Simulate a regeneration path and assert all records are stored with correct linkage/order and valid timestamps for each exchange event.

behaviors.description: If tone regeneration occurs, only the second response is returned to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across modules (generation pipeline -> response selector -> outbound payload). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: behavior does not judge model quality; trigger condition can be forced and responses stubbed. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d): although LLM-involved stages exist, this behavior tests replacement mechanics, not whether model decisions are correct. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text focusing on visibility/selection of drafts, not semantic correctness of model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic doubles for first and second drafts with forced tone-trigger=true. Execute request path and assert customer-facing payload equals second draft and first draft appears only in internal logs/persistence, never outbound.
